texto: But still. It has to end sometime. Wars always do. Everything has to end,' said Josephine, eating another ginger biscuit and getting unexpectedly philosophical. 'Yeah. Things like human civilisation,' I said.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Sophia McDougall: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “But still. It has to end sometime. Wars always do. Everything has to end,' said Josephine, eating…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, civilisation, war pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e war.

frase_original: But still. It has to end sometime. Wars always do. Everything has to end,' said Josephine, eating another ginger biscuit and getting unexpectedly philosophical. 'Yeah. Things like human civilisation,' I said.

texto: What about our human rights,' demanded Carl, who'd gathered a small deputation of kids within minutes. 'There is a WAR ON,' said Crewman Devlin, shortly. I wondered if this meant grown-ups actually listen to you when there wasn't a war on, because somehow I was sceptical.
explicacao: Citação de Sophia McDougall sobre temas ligados a familia. “What about our human rights,' demanded Carl, who'd gathered a small deputation of kids within min…” articula familia, children, human-rights em registro de familia, com relevância para contextos como human-rights e war.
